title: PostgreSQL Primary Database Crash on Production Cluster
symptoms: All application services returning connection refused errors to PostgreSQL
PostgreSQL process terminated with SIGABRT on db-prod-primary-01
WAL corruption errors (PANIC: could not locate valid checkpoint) in database logs
Zero active database connections across all connection poolers
Cascading 5xx errors across all dependent microservices
root_cause: WAL segment corruption caused by a failing NVMe SSD with degrading sectors on the primary database server
resolution: Promoted synchronous standby replica db-prod-standby-01 to primary, reconfigured PgBouncer connection pooler, replaced failed NVMe drive, and rebuilt original server as new standby
preventive_actions: Implement SMART drive monitoring with alerts on reallocated sector count
Add disk I/O latency alerting at p95 greater than 5ms
Deploy automated WAL integrity verification every 6 hours
Schedule quarterly NVMe health audits across database fleet

timeline.event: PostgreSQL primary server crashes, automated PagerDuty alert triggered
On-call engineer Rajesh Kumar acknowledges the P1 alert
Initial assessment confirms database process is down and WAL corruption in logs
Decision made to initiate failover to standby replica
Standby promoted to primary, read replicas reconnecting
All services confirmed operational with normal metrics
Post-failover validation complete, incident marked resolved

embedding_summary: Critical PostgreSQL database crash on production primary server db-prod-primary-01 caused by WAL segment corruption from a failing NVMe SSD. Resulted in 47-minute complete service outage affecting 12,000 users and 340 failed transactions. Resolved by failover to standby replica and NVMe drive replacement.
